metadata.title: Jannis Milz · Software developer, curious by nature
metadata.description: Jannis Milz is a software developer from Switzerland: building eintrittli, coaching gymnastics, and poking at new technologies until they break.

footer.joke: No cookies, no tracking. This paper doesn’t follow you home.

home.headline:
Either I find a way,

or I create one.

letter.p1: Hallo, I’m Jannis, a software developer from Switzerland. The sentence up there has been my life motto for years: I only believe something can’t work once I’ve seriously searched for a way.
letter.p1Note: Also known as stubbornness, but “motto” reads better on a website.
letter.p2: I grew up surrounded by all kinds of technology, so this path was more or less destined. I started programming at twelve; today I do it professionally, as an apprentice software developer at BSI, after two years at the University of Zurich.
letter.pullQuote: “Experiment until it breaks. Then figure out why.”
letter.pullQuoteCaption: My learning method since day one. The breaking comes naturally, the fixing is the skill.
letter.p3: Away from the keyboard I do gymnastics and athletics, more than ten years at my local club by now. These days I coach the younger kids, the way someone once coached me.
letter.p4: It keeps me honest: no code review is as tough a crowd as a group of ten-year-olds.
letter.p5: The rest of my head belongs to whatever last caught my curiosity: biographies, design, psychology, and the occasional unsolvable math problem.
letter.p5Note: Haven’t solved one yet. The name checks out.
letter.p6: This site is the paper edition of all that. Have a look around, and if something sparks a thought, write me: <mail>hi@jannismilz.com</mail>.
letter.ps: PS: This paper is also printed in German, the original edition. The little switch in the masthead changes editions.
letter.figureACaption: Gymnastics, the analog kind of deployment.
letter.figureBPlaceholder: photo from coaching the kids, eventually
letter.figureBCaption: The coaching bench instead of the daily standup.

now.title: Now
now.item1: Building <link>eintrittli.ch</link> with Weborb, a door check-in system so simple it makes clipboards nervous.
now.item3: Competed at SwissSkills 2025 in Web Technologies.

meta.title: Projects
meta.description: Things Jannis Milz has shipped, broken, and shipped again: eintrittli, Weborb, and other projects.

projects.headline: Shipped, broken, and shipped again.
projects.intro: The projects I’m most proud of: from a live product to school projects that taught me why the live product works. More to come.

eintrittli.description: A simple entry check-in system for events and clubs: configurable forms, data export, and deliberately nothing else. No payments, no login, no fuss. Built and run with Weborb.

weborb.description: The small Swiss web studio where my projects grow up and ship: home of eintrittli, and of whatever comes next.

typst-payqr-swiss.description: A Swiss QR bill generator for the Typst typesetting system. Invoices deserve nice typography too.

bbw-heroes.description: A platform where BBW students share their work with others and grow together.

linux-cookbook.description: A guide to the basics of Linux and how to actually use it, written while learning them myself.

swissskills.title: SwissSkills 2025 · Web Technologies
swissskills.body: In 2025 I competed among Switzerland’s best young web developers at SwissSkills. A newspaper even interviewed me about AI; the short version of my answer made it into the headline: my future depends on my development, not on it.
swissskills.figurePlaceholder: photo from the competition floor, eventually

meta.title: Writing
meta.description: Jannis Milz’s own writing, published right here, plus press highlights and an archive of older articles.

writing.headline: What’s been on my mind.
writing.intro: My own thoughts, typeset and published right here. No algorithm, no paywall, just letters.
writing.archiveNote: Mostly from my 2024 Medium era, preserved here like old newspapers.
writing.figureCaption: One desk, a stack of ideas.

notFound.body: In an earlier edition we may have implied this page exists. We regret the error.
